tiles.name: Asakusa
tiles.funFact: Home to Senso-ji, Tokyo's oldest Buddhist temple built in 645 AD!

tiles.name: Ueno
tiles.funFact: Ueno Park has over 1,000 cherry blossom trees and is Tokyo's most popular hanami spot!

tiles.name: Yamanote Line
tiles.funFact: The Yamanote Line completes a full loop around central Tokyo every 59-65 minutes, carrying 3.5 million passengers daily!

tiles.name: Akihabara
tiles.funFact: Known as "Electric Town," Akihabara is the global center of anime, manga, and electronics culture!

tiles.name: Ikebukuro
tiles.funFact: Ikebukuro Station is the world's second-busiest train station, serving over 2.7 million passengers daily!

tiles.name: Shinjuku
tiles.funFact: Shinjuku Station is the busiest train station in the world with over 3.6 million daily passengers!

tiles.name: Harajuku
tiles.funFact: The epicenter of Tokyo's youth fashion culture! Takeshita Street attracts millions of trendsetting visitors.

tiles.name: Tokyo Electric Power
tiles.funFact: TEPCO powers over 45 million people in the greater Tokyo area, one of the world's largest power grids!

tiles.name: Omotesando
tiles.funFact: Known as Tokyo's Champs-Élysées, lined with zelkova trees and flagship stores by world-renowned architects!

tiles.name: Odaiba
tiles.funFact: Built on reclaimed land in Tokyo Bay, home to teamLab Borderless and a giant Gundam statue!

tiles.name: Tokaido Shinkansen
tiles.funFact: Japan's first bullet train line, connecting Tokyo to Osaka at speeds up to 285 km/h since 1964!

tiles.name: Roppongi
tiles.funFact: Home to Roppongi Hills and Tokyo Midtown, featuring world-class art museums and nightlife!

tiles.name: Ebisu
tiles.funFact: Named after Yebisu Beer, this upscale neighborhood is known for great restaurants and the Tokyo Photographic Art Museum!

tiles.name: Shibuya
tiles.funFact: Shibuya Crossing is the world's busiest pedestrian intersection, with up to 3,000 people crossing at once!

tiles.name: Tsukiji
tiles.funFact: The former site of the world's largest fish market, where a bluefin tuna once sold for $3.1 million!

tiles.name: Ginza
tiles.funFact: Tokyo's most luxurious shopping district! Land prices can reach $100,000 per square meter.

tiles.name: Tokyo Station
tiles.funFact: The beautifully restored 1914 red-brick station serves as the terminus for all Shinkansen lines!

tiles.name: Chuo Line
tiles.funFact: The Chuo Rapid Line is one of Tokyo's most important commuter lines, recognizable by its orange trains!

tiles.name: Marunouchi
tiles.funFact: Tokyo's premier business district, home to major corporations and facing the Imperial Palace!

tiles.name: Nihombashi
tiles.funFact: The historic commercial center of Tokyo, where the famous Nihombashi Bridge marks "kilometer zero" for Japan!

tiles.name: Tokyo Gas
tiles.funFact: Founded in 1885, Tokyo Gas was one of Japan's first modern utility companies!

tiles.name: Roppongi Hills
tiles.funFact: This massive urban complex includes the Mori Art Museum, luxury residences, and a 238-meter observation deck!

tiles.name: Aoyama
tiles.funFact: One of Tokyo's most fashionable neighborhoods, known for high-end boutiques and trendy cafes!

tiles.name: Azabu
tiles.funFact: An exclusive residential area home to many embassies and international schools!

tiles.name: Tokyo Tower
tiles.funFact: Inspired by the Eiffel Tower, this 333-meter landmark has been Tokyo's symbol since 1958!

tiles.name: Sobu Line
tiles.funFact: The Sobu Line connects Tokyo's eastern suburbs and carries over 1 million passengers daily!

tiles.name: Akasaka
tiles.funFact: Home to the State Guest House and many high-end restaurants, including Michelin-starred establishments!

tiles.name: Tokyo Skytree
tiles.funFact: The world's tallest tower at 634 meters! The height represents "Musashi," the historic name for Tokyo.
